workflow.persona: Internal product strategist mapping the annual updating amendment workflow used by a compliance or operations team
workflow.entry_condition: The adviser is approaching its annual updating amendment deadline and needs to refresh Form ADV, brochure disclosures, and filing readiness materials.
workflow.completion_condition: The annual updating amendment is submitted, brochure and notice-filing follow-ups are queued or completed, and the filing package is archived.
workflow_notes: This repo still treats each workflow slice as a locked artifact, not a multi-workflow product surface.
This second slice stays inside RIA compliance but changes the work shape from pre-publication marketing review to periodic filing readiness.
Representative internal artifacts are included as workflow inputs, not as fabricated evidence.
Source evidence is limited to SEC instructions, staff guidance FAQs, risk alerts, and rulemaking releases.
Firm-specific routing, counsel involvement, and filing-calendar ownership are treated as local configuration, not unresolved scoring uncertainty.

observed_inputs.note: Source and artifact inventories are observed or curated workflow inputs. Scores and recommendations are inferred from those inputs.

dimensions.label: Automation fit
dimensions.description: Higher means the step is structured, rules-based, and technically repeatable.

dimensions.label: Human sign-off need
dimensions.description: Higher means a named human owner should retain final authority for the step.

dimensions.label: Liability asymmetry
dimensions.description: Higher means the downside of a wrong answer is materially larger than the efficiency gain.

dimensions.label: Evidence strength
dimensions.description: Higher means the step can be anchored in explicit source material, artifacts, and traceable checks.

source_inventory.title: Form ADV
source_inventory.summary: States that Form ADV filing is mandatory, publicly available through IARD, required annually within 90 days of fiscal year end, and subject to federal liability for intentional misstatements or omissions.

source_inventory.title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
source_inventory.summary: Explains annual versus other-than-annual amendments, filing-fee funding, notice-filing mechanics, handling mistaken filing types, and when advisers must switch registration status after reporting ineligibility.

source_inventory.title: Form ADV - General Instructions
source_inventory.summary: Explains the annual updating amendment deadline, full-form update requirement, brochure material-change filing, other-than-annual amendment triggers, and electronic brochure filing through IARD.

source_inventory.title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
source_inventory.summary: Explains how advisers should file and deliver updated brochures, including annual updating amendment brochure obligations, material-change disclosures, searchable PDF filing, and delivery timing to existing clients.

source_inventory.title: Amendments to Form ADV
source_inventory.summary: Summarizes the 2010 Form ADV Part 2 changes, including annual summary-of-material-change obligations, plain-English brochure disclosure topics, electronic brochure filing, and delivery expectations.

source_inventory.title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
source_inventory.summary: Highlights common adviser deficiencies including inaccurate or untimely regulatory filings, stale compliance manuals, and weak annual-review processes.

source_inventory.title: SEC Adopts Reforms Relating to Investment Advisers Operating Exclusively Through the Internet
source_inventory.summary: Explains the amended internet-adviser exemption, including the need to update Form ADV with an eligibility representation and the consequences if a firm no longer qualifies for SEC registration on that basis.

artifact_inventory.name: Annual amendment input checklist
artifact_inventory.description: Structured request for entity changes, office data, websites, social links, conflicts, disciplinary events, and other annual filing inputs.

artifact_inventory.name: Regulatory AUM and eligibility workpaper
artifact_inventory.description: Calculation workbook and notes supporting SEC or state registration eligibility, internet-adviser status, and any switch or withdrawal implications.

artifact_inventory.name: Brochure redline and summary of material changes
artifact_inventory.description: Draft Part 2A updates, redlines, and the annual summary of material changes prepared for review and filing.

artifact_inventory.name: Delivery and retention log
artifact_inventory.description: Evidence of brochure delivery or offer, retention metadata, and queued post-filing follow-up tasks.

artifact_inventory.name: Disciplinary and conflicts tracker
artifact_inventory.description: Tracker of disciplinary events, conflicts, soft-dollar arrangements, compensation changes, and other disclosure-sensitive developments.

artifact_inventory.name: Annual filing readiness memo
artifact_inventory.description: Memo summarizing changes, unresolved issues, delivery obligations, and recommendation on whether the annual amendment is ready to file.

artifact_inventory.name: Current firm profile tracker
artifact_inventory.description: Current legal-entity details, office roster, website and social-media list, and ownership or control changes since the last filing.

artifact_inventory.name: IARD submission receipts and fee confirmations
artifact_inventory.description: Accepted filing receipt, submission timestamp, fee confirmations, and any returned or corrected filing records.

artifact_inventory.name: State notice-filing and fee matrix
artifact_inventory.description: State-by-state notice-filing requirements, IARD funding needs, and expected fees tied to the annual amendment cycle.

artifact_inventory.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export
artifact_inventory.description: The most recent filed Form ADV and brochure package used as the comparison baseline for the annual update.

summary.first_build_wedge: Automate the annual filing mechanics, assist the disclosure-heavy reconciliation work, and keep final filing execution human.

highest_priority_build_step.step_name: Draft the annual updating amendment and notice checklist

hard_guardrail_step.step_name: Authorize and submit the annual amendment

summary.blocked_pattern: Do not ship autonomous annual amendment certification or IARD submission for this workflow in slice 2.

review_queue.action: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.
Keep any future UI limited to approval support and audit traceability, not autonomous action.

step_results.step_description: Collect the prior filing, current firm profile, annual input checklist, and eligibility workpapers into one normalized packet before any drafting begins.

artifacts.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: The work is repeated, checklist-driven packet assembly with stable annual input categories.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should inspect exceptions, but the packet-building step itself does not need final authority.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: Errors can still be caught downstream before filing, so the immediate downside is more about rework than irreversible harm.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The annual packet can be anchored to explicit filing instructions, annual deadlines, and common examination deficiencies.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: The annual updating amendment requires updates across all applicable form parts and schedules, which supports a standardized input packet.

evidence_notes.source_title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
evidence_notes.claim: The annual filing obligation remains separate from other-than-annual amendments, so assembling one clear annual packet reduces filing-type confusion.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Form ADV is a mandatory annual public filing, which makes complete packet assembly an accuracy-control step rather than optional admin work.

evidence_notes.source_title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC has flagged inaccurate or untimely regulatory filings and stale compliance materials as recurring deficiencies, which favors normalized packet assembly.

step_results.build_implication: Build a structured intake lane that requests annual filing inputs, normalizes them into one packet, and highlights missing items before any reviewer time is spent.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (84) and evidence strength (88) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (28) and liability asymmetry (30).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

step_results.step_name: Reconcile registration basis and profile fields
step_results.step_description: Compare current firm facts against Part 1A registration-basis, office, website, social-media, and eligibility fields before drafting the annual amendment.

artifacts.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Many fields are structured comparisons, but eligibility and profile-edge cases still need reviewer interpretation.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should confirm registration-basis changes because they can affect SEC versus state status and public disclosure.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong eligibility or profile answer can create registration and disclosure problems that are expensive to unwind.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The work can be tied to explicit instruction-level update triggers, FAQ guidance, and current SEC rule changes.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: The instructions distinguish annual and prompt-update items, which makes the comparison set explicit but still leaves interpretation on material inaccuracies.

evidence_notes.source_title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
evidence_notes.claim: The FAQ explains that advisers reporting ineligibility on the annual amendment must switch registration within 180 days and that other-than-annual amendments do not replace the annual filing.

evidence_notes.source_title: SEC Adopts Reforms Relating to Investment Advisers Operating Exclusively Through the Internet
evidence_notes.claim: Internet advisers now must update Form ADV with an eligibility representation, which creates a live edge case for registration-basis review.

evidence_notes.source_title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC has identified inaccurate regulatory filings as a recurring deficiency, which supports keeping profile-field reconciliation in a reviewer-visible lane.

step_results.build_implication: Build assistive comparison tooling that flags changed eligibility and profile fields, but keep the final registration-basis conclusion with a reviewer.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (64) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (72) and liability asymmetry (68).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

step_results.step_description: Review Part 2A narrative disclosures, fee language, conflicts, disciplinary items, and summary of material changes against the firm's current practices.

artifacts.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export
Brochure redline and summary of material changes

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: The disclosure headings are structured, but deciding whether narrative disclosures fairly reflect current practice still requires human judgment.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A senior reviewer should confirm brochure and material-change language because it is client-facing and disclosure-sensitive.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: Misstated brochure disclosures can create examination and client-communication problems that outweigh the efficiency gain from blind automation.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The brochure workflow is strongly anchored in Form ADV instructions and SEC Part 2 guidance, even though the writing remains judgment-heavy.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: Annual updating amendments must refresh Part 2A and include the summary of material changes, which gives this review a clear procedural backbone.

evidence_notes.source_title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC staff explains that the brochure filed with the annual amendment also drives delivery timing to existing clients, which raises the importance of disclosure accuracy.

evidence_notes.source_title: Amendments to Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: The guide ties the brochure to plain-English disclosure of fees, conflicts, disciplinary history, and business practices, which keeps this step reviewer-sensitive.

evidence_notes.source_title: Amendments to Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: The required Part 2 topic structure means a tool can organize draft comparisons and redlines even though a reviewer still needs to judge the final wording.

step_results.build_implication: Build redline and change-detection support for brochure sections, but keep final narrative judgment and disclosure calls in a reviewer-confirmed assist lane.

confidence_breakdown.note: Low separation between the top two modes means this step still needs explicit reviewer judgment.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (58) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (79) and liability asymmetry (74).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Low separation between the top two modes means this step still needs explicit reviewer judgment.

review_actions: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.

step_results.step_name: Draft the annual updating amendment and notice checklist
step_results.step_description: Prefill changed fields, assemble brochure attachments, and generate the annual updating amendment and notice-filing checklist for reviewer confirmation.

artifacts.name: Prior Form ADV and brochure export
Brochure redline and summary of material changes

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: This is structured drafting and checklist generation tied to known items, schedules, and filing mechanics.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should validate the staged filing, but the drafting and checklist work itself is largely repeatable.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A bad prefill is still meaningful, but it can be caught before filing if the staged package remains reviewable.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The step can be grounded in explicit form instructions, brochure-filing rules, fee mechanics, and public filing requirements.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: The instructions define the parts and schedules that must be updated in the annual amendment, which makes prefill and checklist generation highly structured.

evidence_notes.source_title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
evidence_notes.claim: The FAQ explains that annual amendment fees and state notice fees must be funded in IARD before submission, which supports a deterministic filing checklist.

evidence_notes.source_title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: The Part 2 FAQ explains that advisers prepare their own brochure PDF and submit it through IARD, which fits staged document-generation support.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Because the filing becomes public and is used for regulatory purposes, the drafting lane should emphasize traceable staging rather than hidden automation.

step_results.build_implication: Build the first product wedge here: structured field drafting, brochure attachment staging, and fee-aware filing checklists that remain reviewable before submission.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (87) and evidence strength (93) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (38) and liability asymmetry (40).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

step_results.step_name: Prepare the readiness memo and exception list
step_results.step_description: Summarize changes, unresolved issues, brochure-delivery obligations, and escalation items into a memo that tells the signer whether the annual amendment is ready to file.
step_results.human_owner_role: Compliance manager or outsourced compliance lead

artifacts.name: Brochure redline and summary of material changes

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: The memo can be scaffolded from structured findings, but the significance and escalation framing still need human judgment.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A reviewer should own the final readiness recommendation because it synthesizes filing, disclosure, and exception judgment.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A weak readiness memo can let a bad filing reach the signer, so the downside remains meaningfully higher than the speed gain.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The memo can be anchored to explicit filing rules, exam observations, brochure-delivery obligations, and the public nature of the filing.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: The instructions specify the annual and prompt-update obligations that the memo should roll up into a filing-readiness recommendation.

evidence_notes.source_title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
evidence_notes.claim: Recurring SEC findings around inaccurate filings and stale annual reviews support keeping the readiness memo explicit and reviewer-visible.

evidence_notes.source_title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Brochure filing and delivery timing can create concrete post-filing obligations, so the memo should call out required follow-up rather than treat submission as the end of the job.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Because intentional misstatements or omissions can carry federal consequences, unresolved exceptions should be clearly surfaced to the accountable signer.

step_results.build_implication: Build memo-drafting and issue-rollup support, but keep reviewer confirmation prominent because this step still packages judgment for the signer.

confidence_breakdown.note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Assist is the right lane because the step is partially structured (62) but still carries meaningful human sign-off need (78) and liability asymmetry (77).
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Do not let this step resolve the review on its own; it should prepare judgment, not replace it.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Moderate score separation supports the recommendation, but the second-best mode remains plausible.

review_actions: Keep reviewer confirmation prominent because the downside of a bad recommendation is still high.

step_results.step_name: Authorize and submit the annual amendment
step_results.step_description: The accountable signer reviews the final package, confirms accuracy, authorizes fee-funded submission, and files the annual updating amendment through IARD.

artifacts.name: IARD submission receipts and fee confirmations

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: Automation can stage information, but the act of certifying and submitting the filing should not be delegated.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: This is the accountable approval and submission point for a regulatory filing.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: A wrong submission creates public, regulatory, and potentially enforcement-facing consequences with little upside from removing the signer.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: The human gate is strongly grounded in the form instructions, filing mechanics, and the SEC's statements about the filing's consequences.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Form ADV is mandatory, public, and subject to federal liability for intentional misstatements or omissions, which makes autonomous submission a poor fit.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: Failure to update Form ADV as required can lead to registration consequences, which reinforces the need for a named accountable signer.

evidence_notes.source_title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
evidence_notes.claim: The FAQ ties submission to funded IARD accounts, filing type choices, and registration-status consequences, all of which are reviewer-signoff sensitive.

evidence_notes.source_title: Frequently Asked Questions on Form ADV and IARD
evidence_notes.claim: Even when a firm has filed interim amendments, the SEC still expects the distinct annual updating amendment, which makes the execution step a conscious filing decision rather than a background sync.

step_results.build_implication: Keep execution and submission human. Product work here should stop at approval support, fee checks, and audit-ready packet assembly.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Keep Human is mandatory because this step is an explicit supervisory approval point.
decision_rationale.why_not_automate: Automation here should only package context and evidence for the accountable reviewer.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

review_actions: Keep any future UI limited to approval support and audit traceability, not autonomous action.

step_results.step_name: Archive receipts, delivery evidence, and follow-ups
step_results.step_description: Store filing receipts, brochure-delivery evidence, notice-filing confirmations, and queued follow-up tasks in the annual books-and-records package.

artifacts.name: Brochure redline and summary of material changes
IARD submission receipts and fee confirmations

automation_fit.label: Automation fit
automation_fit.rationale: This is structured packaging, timestamp capture, and follow-up creation after the filing is complete, but firm-specific recordkeeping variations still matter.

human_signoff_need.label: Human sign-off need
human_signoff_need.rationale: A person may inspect exceptions, but the default recordkeeping and task-queue work is mechanical.

liability_asymmetry.label: Liability asymmetry
liability_asymmetry.rationale: The main risk is incomplete records or missed delivery tasks, which can usually be corrected if surfaced promptly.

evidence_strength.label: Evidence strength
evidence_strength.rationale: Archival and follow-up tasks are well anchored in brochure-delivery guidance, filing instructions, and SEC books-and-records expectations.

evidence_notes.source_title: Form ADV - General Instructions
evidence_notes.claim: The annual amendment workflow includes brochure filing and update obligations, which supports structured capture of receipts and retained records after submission.

evidence_notes.source_title: Staff Responses to Questions About Part 2 of Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: Updated brochures filed with the annual amendment can trigger delivery to existing clients, so post-filing follow-up should be systematically logged.

evidence_notes.source_title: Amendments to Form ADV
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC's brochure guidance ties annual material-change communication to delivery obligations, which makes delivery evidence part of the annual package rather than an afterthought.

evidence_notes.source_title: The Five Most Frequent Compliance Topics Identified in OCIE Examinations of Investment Advisers
evidence_notes.claim: The SEC has highlighted books-and-records and stale compliance processes as common deficiencies, which supports automating archival completeness checks and follow-up creation.

step_results.build_implication: Build automated archival packaging, receipt capture, and follow-up task creation so the filing cycle ends with a clean books-and-records bundle instead of manual cleanup.

confidence_breakdown.note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

decision_rationale.why_this_mode: Automate is the best fit because automation fit (79) and evidence strength (86) comfortably outweigh human sign-off need (28) and liability asymmetry (30).
decision_rationale.why_not_more_human: Human review should stay focused on exceptions, not default packet handling.
decision_rationale.confidence_note: Clear score separation supports the current recommendation.

build_decisions.decision: Build automation around packet assembly, structured field drafting, notice-filing prep, and post-filing archival before expanding reviewer UI.
build_decisions.rationale: These steps are repeated, rules-based filing mechanics with strong instruction-level anchors and lower downside when a human still reviews the final packet.

build_decisions.decision: Build reviewer aids for registration-basis checks, brochure/material-change reconciliation, and readiness memo preparation instead of autonomous filing judgment.
build_decisions.rationale: These steps combine structured data with disclosure and eligibility judgment that should stay visible to a compliance reviewer.

build_decisions.decision: Do not automate final certification or IARD submission in slice 2.
build_decisions.rationale: The execution step is a regulatory filing with public-disclosure and misstatement consequences, so automation should only stage evidence for the accountable signer.

explicit_defers: No reviewer UI beyond this published payload
